title: Finish the Arkansas Valley Conduit Act
short_title: Finish the Arkansas Valley Conduit Act
plain_summary: This law is about a water pipeline project in Colorado called the Arkansas Valley Conduit. The pipeline will bring water from a reservoir to communities in the Arkansas River Valley. The law makes it easier for these communities to pay for the pipeline by reducing how much they have to pay and giving them more time to pay it back. Communities will pay thirty five percent of the total cost and have up to seventy five years to pay, with lower interest rates if they have money problems.
last_action_text: The Chair directed the Clerk to notify the Senate of the action of the House.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Finish the Arkansas Valley Conduit Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Arkansas Valley Conduit, Colorado
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called Public Law eighty seven dash five ninety. The changes remove some language about payment requirements for the Arkansas Valley Conduit and replace it with new rules. The Arkansas Valley Conduit is a water pipeline project in Colorado. Under the new rules, the conduit will help provide drinking water to communities and homes that do not have reliable water supplies. The contract for building this conduit will require payment of thirty five percent of the total cost, even if other water laws say something different. These payments will come from two sources. First, any money provided by groups other than the federal government during construction. Second, if the Secretary determines there is financial hardship, the remaining balance can be paid back over up to seventy five years. The interest rate will be half of whatever rate the Treasury Secretary sets under a different part of the law. This can include money earned from contracts that use extra capacity in the pipeline or exchange contracts using facilities from the Fryingpan Arkansas project. The contract will also require the parties who sign it to take care of operating, maintaining, and replacing the conduit. The section also makes a small change to refer to the correct part of the law.
